Scenario.title: Transportation Systems Phishing and SCADA Disruption

overview.Summary: A skilled threat actor initiates an attack on a transportation system through a phishing campaign, leading to unauthorized access and subsequent disruption of SCADA systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 164 Name: Mobile Phishing Description: Targets mobile users with phishing for sensitive info.
stage.description: The attack begins with a phishing campaign targeting employees of the transportation company. Employees receive emails containing malicious links designed to steal credentials, exploiting CVE-2024-43047. This initial access leverages the Web vector, focusing on internet-facing assets like employee email accounts.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 467 Name: Cross Site Identification Description: Harvests identifying info via active sessions on social sites.
stage.description: Once inside the network, the attacker uses Mimikatz to extract credentials from compromised endpoints. This execution phase focuses on internal network assets, utilizing the Network vector to propagate and gather necessary permissions for further actions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 440 Name: Hardware Integrity Attack Description: Exploits system maintenance processes to alter hardware components.
stage.description: The final stage disrupts SCADA systems controlling transportation infrastructure. By compromising these systems directly, the attacker halts operations, achieving the intended impact through targeted attacks on critical industrial assets.

Scenario.summary: The attack sequence starts with a phishing email to gain initial access, followed by credential extraction using Mimikatz, and culminates in disrupting SCADA systems to disable transportation operations, demonstrating a clear progression from entry to system compromise and operational disruption.
